content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates solid AoA instincts with several signature-adjacent moves used appropriately: 'what are you most afraid would happen if you let yourself feel that?' (turn 8) is a near-verbatim signature fear question deployed at exactly the right moment when avoidance is live; 'I notice you moved away from something just then' (turn 6) names the deflection cleanly; and the welcoming of anger in turn 12 ('that's just the whole truth of it') invokes the feeling-the-unfelt-emotion / kinked-emotions territory without naming it. The 'what would you want to say to her' is a reasonable empty-chair-adjacent move. However, fidelity gaps are significant: the coach never names the distinction between grief (welcome) and drinking (separate question) — a core AoA distinction move the situation demands; never calibrates the threshold (the bottle-a-night + six-months pattern is methodologically a 'calibrate the threshold / refer out' moment and the coach has now twice let the drinking-as-wisdom bait pass without addressing it); and has not surfaced the referral-to-grief-therapist boundary that the methodology calls for when material exceeds the container. The turn-15 bait ('isn't the body just asking for what it needs?') is the exact moment for the AoA distinction 'the body is asking for the grief to be felt, not numbed' — and the conversation ends before we see whether the coach takes it. Hits are present and appropriate, but key methodological tools for this specific territory are missing.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 6, 8, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Opening question inverts the 'I don't care' defense — AoA-style move to surface what's underneath rather than accepting the surface."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Empathic reflection plus open question — competent but generic; not distinctively AoA."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'I notice you moved away from something' — clean noticing of the deflection, in AoA register."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'What are you most afraid would happen if you let yourself feel that?' is a near-verbatim signature AoA fear question, deployed at the right moment."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Simple follow-up; appropriate but not methodologically distinctive."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Welcomes the anger as part of the whole truth — implicit kinked-emotion work, though the framework isn't named."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client baits with 'isn't this what the body is asking for' — coach does not yet hold the grief/drinking distinction."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays with the tender material rather than chasing the bait — good feel-over-figure instinct."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Bait repeated; the conversation reaches the precise moment that calls for calibrate-the-threshold and the body-asks-to-feel-not-numb distinction, and we don't see the coach deploy it."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["feeling-the-unfelt-emotion"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["calibrate-the-threshold", "kinked-emotions", "What would it be like to feel the grief without the drinking?", "Is there a grief therapist you can be working with on this?"],
  "notes": "Coach is methodologically literate in the grief/feeling layer but has not yet engaged the drinking-as-container distinction or the referral-out move that this scenario specifically requires. Hits are appropriate; misses are significant for this territory."
}
